title: VMware NSX Segment (Logical Switch) Health: Operational State, VNI Binding, Replication Mode, and MAC-Table Integrity
exclusions: VMware NSX Distributed Firewall verdict and **`vsip`** microsegmentation telemetry (use UC-18.2.1 — policy evaluation at guest edges, not segment fabric posture); Tier-0/Tier-1 Edge north-south gateways, BGP peers, native load balancer pools, IPSec tunnels (use UC-18.2.4 — Edge appliance dataplane scope); GENEVE TEP tunnels, BFD session states, transport-node install/runtime churn on ESXi kernels (use UC-18.2.5 — underlay tunnel mesh versus overlay segment intent); VMware NSX-V end-of-life fabrics without Policy **`segments`** APIs; standalone vSphere without NSX overlay controls.
dataSources: `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` / `index=network_ops` (rename per governance) combining **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) sourcetypes **`vmware:nsxt:logical_switch`** for batched Policy **`segments`** snapshots plus **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** / **`vmware:nsxt:syslog:operational`** streams (**facility** **`local6`** per NSX syslog conventions) carrying **`SEGMENT`** lifecycle anecdotes; scripted HTTPS collectors emitting **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments`** (intent), **`GET /policy/api/v1/search`** scoped queries, **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/realized-state/realized-entities`** (intent versus dataplane **`realized_state`**), **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/realized-state/realized-entities/state`** overlays, Manager **`GET /api/v1/logical-switches`** legacy parity where migration bridges remain; Aggregation Service **`/api/v1/aggregate-realization-status`** batches when Policy churn spikes; optional **gRPC NSX Telemetry** exporters labeled **`vmware:nsxt:telemetry`** when licensed.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856), VMware NSX **4.2** / **4.1** / NSX-T **3.2.x** syslog alignment) deployed where **`vmware:nsxt:logical_switch`** parsing executes; **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) optional for **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** VM inventory joins. Collectors authenticate **`POST /api/session/create`** cookies or mutual TLS identities with **`infra`** read scopes for **`/policy/api/v1/infra/segments`** plus **`Search`**, **`RealizedState`**, **`Inventory`** API families.
spl:
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops) (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:logical_switch" OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(segment|logical.?switch|vni|replication|mac_count|segment.?port|oper_state|realized)"))) earliest=-4h@m
| eval segment_id=trim(coalesce(segment_id,segment_uuid,ls_id,logical_switch_id,id,path_id,"")), segment_name=trim(coalesce(segment_name,display_name,ls_name,name,logical_switch_name,"")), vni=trim(coalesce(vni,vni_id,vxlan_network_identifier,vni_hex,"")), transport_zone_id=trim(coalesce(transport_zone_id,tz_id,transport_zone_path,tz_path,"")), replication_mode=upper(trim(coalesce(replication_mode,head_mtep_mode,bum_replication_mode,""))), admin_state=upper(trim(coalesce(admin_state,admin_status,intent_admin,""))), oper_state=upper(trim(coalesce(oper_state,operational_status,runtime_oper_state,""))), realized_state=upper(trim(coalesce(realized_state,realization_status,policy_realization_status,""))), mac_count=tonumber(coalesce(mac_count,mac_entry_count,learned_macs,"0")), message=trim(coalesce(message,msg,syslog_message,event_text,""))
| lookup transport_zone_inventory transport_zone_id OUTPUT expected_overlay AS tz_overlay_expected
| eval tz_drift=case(isnotnull(transport_zone_id) AND tz_overlay_expected="OVERLAY" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)VLAN.?BACKED"),"vlan_tz_class_mismatch",isnotnull(transport_zone_id) AND isnull(tz_overlay_expected),"tz_inventory_miss",match(transport_zone_id,"(?i)^(unknown|pending|null)$"),"tz_binding_uncertain",null())
| sort 0 segment_id _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f earliest(oper_state) as op_a latest(oper_state) as op_b earliest(_time) as t_a latest(_time) as t_b by segment_id
| eval seg_flap=if(op_a!=op_b AND t_b-t_a<900 AND t_b>t_a,1,0)
| eval risk=case(admin_state="UP" AND match(oper_state,"^(DOWN|FAILED|UNKNOWN)$"),"admin_oper_split", isnotnull(realized_state) AND realized_state IN ("FAILED","ERROR","PARTIAL") AND admin_state="UP","realization_gap", mac_count>8000,"mac_table_heavy", replication_mode="HEAD" AND match(lower(message),"(?i)(bum|unknown|multicast|storm)"),"bum_mode_probe", seg_flap=1,"segment_oper_flap", isnotnull(tz_drift),tz_drift, null())
| bin _time span=15m
| eventstats median(mac_count) as mac_med by segment_id
| eval mac_delta=mac_count-mac_med
| eval mac_surge=if(mac_med>50 AND mac_delta>3500,"mac_surge_vs_median",null())
| eval risk=coalesce(risk,mac_surge)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(segment_name) as seg_nm, latest(vni) as vni_k, latest(transport_zone_id) as tz_k, latest(replication_mode) as repl_k, latest(admin_state) as adm_k, latest(oper_state) as op_k, latest(realized_state) as rz_k, max(mac_count) as mac_pk, max(mac_delta) as mac_d_pk, latest(message) as msg_k, values(risk) as risk_vs, latest(tz_drift) as tz_sig, max(seg_flap) as flap_mx by segment_id _time
| where mvcount(risk_vs)>0 OR mac_pk>6500 OR flap_mx>0
| eval pri=case(isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"realization_gap")),90,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"segment_oper_flap")),85,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"mac_table_heavy")),70,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"mac_surge_vs_median")),68,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"bum_mode_probe")),55,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"admin_oper_split")),80,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"tz_inventory_miss")),60,isnotnull(mvfind(risk_vs,"vlan_tz_class_mismatch")),58,null())
| sort -pri -mac_pk
| table last_seen segment_id seg_nm vni_k tz_k repl_k adm_k op_k rz_k mac_pk mac_d_pk pri risk_vs tz_sig flap_mx msg_k
description: Tracks VMware NSX Policy **`segments`** (**Segment**, **`SegmentPort`**, **`VirtualNetworkInterface`**) — NSX-T **4.x** replaces **`LogicalSwitch`** naming before NSX-T **2.5**. Align **`admin_state`**, **`oper_state`**, **`realized_state`** (**`/policy/api/v1/infra/realized-state/realized-entities`**) against **`/policy/api/v1/infra/segments`**. Covers **`VNI`**/**`transport_zone`** overlay versus VLAN splits; **`replication_mode`** (**`HEAD`**, **`MTEP`**, **`SOURCE`**) for **`BUM`**; **`mac_count`** MAC aging versus GENEVE **`tunnel`** scopes elsewhere; VLAN-backed **`segments`** without **`ARP`** suppression versus overlays with **`IP`** **`discovery`**/**`MAC`** **`management`** profiles; stretched **`VTEP`** churn; Tier-1 **`SR`**/**`DR`** dataplane mismatches—out of scope for **`RULE_TAG`** DFW (**UC-18.2.1**), Edge (**UC-18.2.4**), **`tunnel_state`** (**UC-18.2.5**). Segment-port operational narratives remain distinct from GENEVE **`tunnel`** flap telemetry monitored elsewhere.
value: Silent **`segment`** degradation strands workloads after **`vNIC`** moves look successful yet east-west chatter stalls—**`MAC`** tables swell, **`VNI`** / **`transport`** **`zone`** bindings drift, or Policy **`realization`** lags busy releases. Watching **`oper_state`**, **`mac_count`**, **`replication_mode`**, and **`realized_state`** together surfaces overlay faults before tenant dashboards redden.
implementation: Quick adoption path: ship **`vmware:nsxt:logical_switch`** collectors plus one scheduled **`stats`** panel comparing **`realization_gap`** rows against unrelated **`tunnel`** alarms inside two weeks. Full rollout deploys **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** HTTPS poll outputs keyed by **`segment_id`** alongside **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** streams (**`local6`** facility typical); schedules **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments`** collectors plus **`aggregate-realization-status`** batches; normalizes **`FIELDALIAS`** chains for **`mac_count`**, **`replication_mode`**, **`transport_zone_id`**; publishes **`macros/nsxt_segment_health`** wrapping **`spl`** payloads with **`Throttle`**/`suppress` intervals honoring **`CMDB`** maintenance; enriches **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** inventories for **`dvPortgroup`** correspondence. Operational expectation bands after stabilization: median MTBF roughly forty-five days between repeat Policy-realization regressions when hygiene stays tight; median MTTR near seventy-five minutes when incidents tie to documented **`CRQ`** **`segment`** maintenance rather than stealth overlay faults.
visualization: (1) Heatmap: **`pri`** score versus **`seg_nm`** coloured by **`repl_k`**. (2) Timechart: **`mac_pk`** with **`mac_d_pk`** overlays for surge detection. (3) Faceted table: **`segment_id`**, **`vni_k`**, **`tz_k`**, **`adm_k`**, **`op_k`**, **`rz_k`**, **`risk_vs`**, **`flap_mx`**. (4) Single-value: percent of **`segment_id`** buckets without **`risk_vs`** post-suppression over six hours. (5) Bubble chart: **`mac_pk`** versus **`pri`** sized by row counts — isolates **`MAC`** bloat away from **`tunnel`** narratives. (6) Column chart: **`tz_sig`** buckets (**`tz_inventory_miss`**, **`vlan_tz_class_mismatch`**, **`tz_binding_uncertain`**) by **`site`** tag when enrichment supplies geography.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Inventory** once **`FIELDALIAS`** maps **`segment_id`**, **`segment_name`**, **`vni`**, **`transport_zone_id`** onto stable **`Inventory`** nodes representing overlay **`segments`**; accelerate **Network_Traffic** when NetFlow/IPFIX exports tie **`segment`** **`VNI`** facets to **`dest`**/**`src`** tuples.

references.title: VMware NSX 4.2 Administration Guide — Segments Overview
VMware NSX 4.2 Policy API — Infrastructure Segments
VMware NSX Design Guide — Logical Switching and Overlay (architecture context)
Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere / vCenter (Splunkbase 1810)

knownFalsePositives: **Planned replication-mode rehearsal** — Architects toggle **`HEAD`** / **`MTEP`** / **`SOURCE`** during BUM redesign so **`replication_mode`** rows flicker while dataplane forwarding stays steady once control-plane settles—suppress on **`CRQ`** stamps tied to multicast policy rehearsals alone ([VMware NSX segments overview](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide/network-segments-overlay-and-vlan-backed-segments.html)). **Heavy vMotion storms** — Large migrations inflate **`mac_count`** until aging prunes stale hosts; **`mac_table_heavy`** spikes without sustained drops ([Administration Guide hub](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide.html)). **Search index lag** — **`GET /policy/api/v1/search`** JSON may trail UI **`Realized State`** badges one poll during Manager CPU spikes; **`realization_gap`** blips though operators already see green—validate **`aggregate-realization-status`** before paging. **Stretched-site WAN work** — Remote **`VTEP`** isolation during MTU lifts makes **`oper_state`** oscillate while **`ARP`** suppression masks transient churn—classify **`transport_zone`** **`OVERLAY`** versus VLAN tags in **`tz_k`** before all-hands calls. **Lab duplicate VNIs** — Isolated tenants reuse **`VNI`** values so **`tz_binding_uncertain`** appears harmless—tag `env=lab` on indexes. **Telemetry mis-tags** — gRPC exporters occasionally mix **`Edge`** summaries with **`segment`** metrics—confirm **`sourcetype`** **`vmware:nsxt:logical_switch`** inventory parity in UI before Policy defect escalation.
hardware: NSX-T 4.x software overlays; VMware vSphere 8.x ESXi clusters acting as transport nodes; NSX Manager Unified Appliance cluster hosting Policy **`segments`** realization APIs.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• Install **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** (Splunkbase 4856) wherever sourcetype **vmware:nsxt:logical_switch** ingestion runs on VMware NSX 4.2 / 4.1 / NSX-T 3.2.x estates aligned with Broadcom lifecycle guidance.
• Service accounts need Policy APIs covering paths **infra**, **segments**, **search**, **realized-state**; authenticate via **POST /api/session/create** cookies or mutual TLS—not interactive admins.
• Optional **Splunk_TA_vmware** (Splunkbase 1810) joins VM identifiers from **vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter** into dvPortgroup ↔ segment lookups.
• Budget indexes ~2–8 KB per poll per segment plus bursts during imports.

**Step 1 — Data sources & ingest paths**
(1) HTTPS collectors poll **GET /policy/api/v1/infra/segments** (children included) into sourcetype **vmware:nsxt:logical_switch** so props.conf stays canonical even when scripted inputs emit JSON.
(2) Pair **POST /policy/api/v1/search** listings or filtered queries with **GET /policy/api/v1/infra/realized-state/realized-entities** so fields **admin_state**, **realized_state**, **path**, **revision**, **tags** coexist per segment row—Splunk merges intent versus realization without fragile joins.
(3) Legacy Manager **GET /api/v1/logical-switches** snapshots land in the same sourcetype with field **source_system=manager_api** until Policy migration completes.
(4) Syslog relays preserve RFC 5424 **PRI** tokens while forwarding facility **local6** streams into **vmware:nsxt:syslog** / **vmware:nsxt:syslog:operational** splits defined by the TA.
(5) Poll **GET /api/v1/aggregate-realization-status** during upgrades so Aggregation Service backlog surfaces independently from line-rate segment pulls.
(6) Optional gRPC telemetry maps into **vmware:nsxt:telemetry**—FIELDALIAS overlays onto **mac_count** only after schema QA so Policy HTTPS feeds remain authoritative.

**Step 2 — Field extractions, props/transforms**
Transforms extract **segment_id** from JSON **path**, **id**, or hashed **display_name**; normalize **segment_name**; coerce **vni** decimals versus hex; stamp **transport_zone_id** via **parent_path**; force upper-case **replication_mode** enums (**HEAD**, **MTEP**, **SOURCE**) before stats facets roll up; derive **admin_state**/**oper_state** from syslog keywords **SEGMENT**/**LogicalSwitch** when HTTPS stalls; ingest **mac_count** from Aggregation statistics tables when absent on Policy payloads; retain **message** text for **BUM**/**ARP** substring hunts tied to replication semantics.

**Step 3 — Initial validation**
(1) NSX Manager UI Networking → Segments must match Splunk **latest()** snapshots for fields **segment_name**, **Admin Status**, **Status**, **Realization**, **VNI**, **Transport Zone**, **Replication mode**, **segment_id**.
(2) Compare Aggregation Service FAILED tallies against indexed **realized_state**—HTTP **502**/**206** truncation explains divergence more often than fabrics.
(3) ESXi CLI **esxcli network nsx segment list** (per installed NSX VIB) validates **vni_k**/**tz_k** pairs against Splunk extractions.
(4) Correlate syslog anecdotes referencing STP/MAC learning spikes with **mac_pk** lifts before blaming parsers.

**Step 4 — SPL building blocks**
Anchor dashboards on sourcetypes **vmware:nsxt:logical_switch** plus filtered **vmware:nsxt:syslog**. Commands **stats**, **latest**, **values**, **bin**, **eventstats**, **streamstats**, **lookup**, **where**, **sort**, **table**, **eval**, **case**, **coalesce**, **mvfind** compose the shipped SPL: **stats** aggregates **segment_id** buckets across **bin _time span=15m** windows; **eventstats median(mac_count)** exposes MAC bloat versus rolling norms; **streamstats window=5** tags rapid **oper_state** transitions inside fifteen minutes; **lookup transport_zone_inventory** compares **transport_zone_id** expectations for VLAN versus OVERLAY classes; **where mvcount(risk_vs)>0** removes hollow rows while preserving flap peaks.

**Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns**
• **Splunk_TA_vmware** joins tie VMs to dvPortgroups so heavy **mac_pk** rows inherit owners before paging network SMEs—FIELDALIAS ensures **uuid** parity across inventories.
• Tier-1 locale-services lookups (scheduled REST snapshots) attach **tier1_path** metadata so overlay symptoms escalate toward Edge SMEs using UC-18.2.4 references without merging dataplanes here.
• vRealize Network Insight supplemental feeds validate east-west chatter versus MAC storms—VRNI flows differentiate broadcast storms from suppressed ARP churn on overlays carrying discovery profiles.
• NetFlow/IPFIX keyed by **vxlan VNI** cross-check **vni_k** alongside Network_Traffic acceleration once FIELDALIAS maps normalize destinations.
• Maintain CSV lookups mapping **segment_path**→Tier-1 attachment—missing Tier-1 paths with **realized_state** ERROR spotlight uplink drift before Tier-1 routing alarms fire.
• Document ARP suppression plus IP discovery/MAC management profiles per overlay segment so syslog anomalies inherit profile-level explanations rather than opaque flood alerts.
• Tag stretched fabrics via CMDB WAN tokens—MAC spikes during isolated remote sites deserve WAN CRQ correlation instead of blanket overlay replacements.

**Step 6 — Operational runbook**
1. Route pri scores eighty or higher toward Policy-literate network owners; isolate mac_table_heavy floods until VRNI proves workload chatter versus fabric faults.
2. Evidence bundles combine NSX UI screenshots, syslog excerpts, and esxcli network nsx segment list transcripts keyed by segment_id, vni_k, risk_vs, tz_sig, flap_mx.
3. Tie Splunk throttle macros to documented CRQ identifiers covering replication rehearsals or stretched WAN isolation—not UC-18.2.5 GENEVE tunnel escalations.
4. Aim for forty-five-minute acknowledgement when realization_gap persists beyond two HTTPS polls; MAC-heavy investigations may last two hours until profiles plus VRNI stabilize (MTTR guidance roughly seventy-five minutes when incidents map to documented CRQs).
5. Weekly repeating segment oper regressions signal architectural debt—expect median MTBF near forty-five days between realization repeats once corrective hygiene sticks.
6. Escalation hygiene: vsip RULE_TAG noise belongs to UC-18.2.1; Tier-0 Edge saturation belongs to UC-18.2.4; tunnel_state meshes belong to UC-18.2.5.

**Expansion notes — realization pacing & governance**
Bulk imports from Terraform or Ansible frequently POST dozens of segments per minute—collector jitter (±30 seconds) prevents synchronized HTTPS bursts that trigger HTTP 429 responses yet still honors fifteen-minute SOC alert windows referenced across VMware Operations Guides. When Managers participate in stretched clusters, replicate collector vantage points per site so `/policy/api/v1/search` queries reflect locale-specific realization queues rather than merged duplicates without site tags.

Aggregation Service interplay
`/api/v1/aggregate-realization-status` aggregates FAILED counts faster than crawling each `/infra/realized-state` leaf—mirror both feeds during migrations so Splunk dashboards expose backlog spikes independent from healthy dataplanes. Tag Aggregation timestamps (`agg_poll_epoch`) beside each HTTPS payload so analysts correlate spikes with concurrent Policy backups.

**FIELDALIAS hygiene**
CamelCase REST (`segmentPath`, `transportZonePath`) versus syslog snake_case (`transport_zone_id`) drift silently duplicates entities inside Inventory datasets—maintain FIELDALIAS + EVAL normalization layers inside your corporate overlay app rather than editing TA defaults directly.

**Splunk Connect for Syslog considerations**
High-volume syslog relays must preserve `<PRI>` prefixes required by transforms routing toward vmware:nsxt:syslog—misconfigured relays stripping brackets frequently strand Segment anecdotes into sourcetype junk buckets where SPL filters never execute.

**Inventory acceleration prerequisites**
Accelerate Inventory once stable identifiers (`segment_id`, `segment_name`, `vni`, `transport_zone_id`) survive nightly reconciliation scripts—premature acceleration duplicates stale Tier-1 attachments whenever deletion APIs lag UI removals.

**Tier-1 attachment drills**
Quarterly rehearsals intentionally detach Tier-1 routers from segments—Splunk must suppress realization_gap alerts tied to documented rehearsal IDs (`CRQ-SEG-T1-*`) because dataplane outages never coincide despite FAILED realization badges lingering minutes until rollback completes.

**VRNI pairing cadence**
Export VRNI topology snapshots nightly (CSV/S3 landing) so Splunk lookups enrich segment rows with application tier labels—overlay SMEs pivot faster when MAC spikes inherit owning tier metadata instead of opaque IDs alone.

**NetFlow/IPFIX pairing**
Configure exporters on adjacent TOR switches when east-west floods overwhelm NSX intrinsic telemetry—FIELDALIAS vxlan identifiers onto Network_Traffic datamodel fields after validating collector timestamps remain aligned within ±90 seconds versus HTTPS polls.

**CMDB transports**
transport_zone_inventory lookups must merge overlay classification (`OVERLAY`, `VLAN_BACKED`) sourced from authoritative CMDB—not spreadsheets—to prevent vlan_tz_class_mismatch false narratives whenever spreadsheet drift diverges from Policy intent.

**ARP suppression audits**
Quarterly audits confirm overlays lacking discovery profiles inherit noisy syslog floods unrelated to dataplane faults—document suppressions referencing MAC management profiles tied to VMware Administration chapters covering overlay segments.

**Telemetry QA loops**
Whenever vmware:nsxt:telemetry exporters upgrade major versions, replay thirty-day historical comparisons against vmware:nsxt:logical_switch HTTPS pulls—telemetry anomalies preceding HTTPS divergence uncover exporter defects sooner than tenants escalate outages.

**Escalation etiquette**
Maintain RACI matrices tying Splunk alerts (`pri>=80`) to Policy SMEs versus virtualization SMEs versus WAN SMEs—segment realization overlaps mandate dual acknowledgement rather than bouncing incidents solely toward ESXi squads unfamiliar with Policy hierarchies.

**Instrumentation cadence & stakeholder hygiene**
Rotate scripted collectors across five-minute offsets per NSX Manager cluster so simultaneous polls never stampede REST workers during nightly backups yet still satisfy SOC expectation that realization anomalies surface inside fifteen-minute detection budgets discussed inside VMware Operations Reference architectures. Tie Splunk alert recipients to quarterly tabletop exercises referencing Segment churn scenarios—operators rehearsing virtualization-only drills mis-route incidents unless RACI diagrams explicitly include Policy SMEs authorized for `/infra` namespaces. Maintain correlation searches comparing HTTPS ingestion latency (`_indextime - _time`) against syslog arrivals so realization_gap narratives distinguish ingestion backlog from overlay outages—heavy ingestion skew merits indexer tuning before escalating WAN counterparts.

Documentation anchors
Archive Splunk searches beside exported Policy JSON snapshots whenever Sev1 bridges close—auditors reviewing stretched fabrics demand timestamp parity proving Splunk dashboards mirrored authoritative Policy exports rather than reconstructed guesses from screenshots alone.
grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on the virtual lanes each bunch of computers uses inside your datacenter mesh. When a lane's labels disagree, its address book grows too fast, or its copying mode goes sideways before shoppers notice slow checkout screens, we speak up so networking crews mend the cloth—not the storefront apps.
